==Season review==

===August 2019===

Preparations for the 2020 season started with the announcement from chairman Andrew Chalmers that the Bulls would move away from [[Odsal Stadium]] for the upcoming season due to the high rent and maintenance costs, with [[Dewsbury Rams]] home ground [[Crown Flatt]] hosting Bradford's home games.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49365567|title=Bradford Bulls: Odsal departure confirmed after 1 September to take up Dewsbury tenancy|work=BBC Sport |date=15 August 2019 |accessdate=28 October 2019}}</ref> Following this news the [[Rugby Football League]] placed the Bulls under 'special measures' over concerns of the club's finances and budgets for 2020 which also prevented Bradford from making signings during this period.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/bradford-bulls-in-special-measures-as-move-to-dewsbury-rams-allowed-for-one-season-only-following-odsal-exit-1-9954705|title=Bradford Bulls in "special measures" as move to Dewsbury Rams allowed for one season only following Odsal exit|work=BBC Sport |date=23 August 2019 |accessdate=28 October 2019}}</ref> Loose forward [[George Milton (rugby league)|George Milton]] and second-row [[Colton Roche]] left the club sometime during the season.

===September 2019===

Academy product and prolific try-scorer [[Ethan Ryan]] signed a two-year deal with [[Super League]] side [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17900363.andrew-chalmers-ins-outs-bulls-squad-revealed/?ref=ar|title=ANDREW CHALMERS: The ins and outs of Bulls squad revealed|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=13 September 2019 |accessdate=28 October 2019}}</ref> Following Ryan's departure head coach [[John Kear]] announced that [[Matt Garside]], [[Matty Wildie]], [[Ashley Gibson]], [[Callum Bustin]] and [[James Green (rugby league)|James Green]] would not be offered new contracts at the club and were released.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17906665.kear-39-heartache-39-bradford-bulls-departures/|title=Kear 'heartache' at Bradford Bulls departures|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=17 September 2019 |accessdate=28 October 2019}}</ref> Garside subsequently signed for local rivals [[Halifax R.L.F.C.]] whilst Green signed for [[York City Knights]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.halifaxrlfc.co.uk/article/53376/garside-inks-fax-deal|title=Garside Inks Fax Deal|work=Halifax RLFC|date=30 September 2019|accessdate=28 October 2019|archive-date=28 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191028231448/https://www.halifaxrlfc.co.uk/article/53376/garside-inks-fax-deal|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17952740.york-sign-prop-james-green-bradford-bulls/|title=York sign prop James Green from Bradford Bulls|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=7 October 2019 |accessdate=28 October 2019}}</ref> Hooker Wildie signed for [[Leigh Centurions]] and prop Bustin switched codes to play rugby union for [[Yorkshire Carnegie]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/paper-talk-new-york-target-nrl-stars-salford-keen-on-ikahihifo-ellis-set-for-leigh-return/|title=Paper Talk|work=Love Rugby League |date=28 October 2019 |accessdate=28 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17917806.kear-backs-former-bull-bustin-union-quot-return-quot/|title=Kear backs former Bull Bustin for union "return"|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=21 September 2019 |accessdate=28 October 2019}}</ref>

===October 2019===

As fears grew over the financial stability of the club it was announced that a senior player evoked a termination of his contract relating to issues revolving around pension payments.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/bradford-player-evokes-contract-termination-over-pension-dispute/|title=Bradford player evokes contract termination over pension dispute|work=Total Rugby League |date=4 October 2019 |accessdate=28 October 2019}}</ref> Throughout the month there were numerous reports that the Bulls were going to be sold to a 'UK investor group' spearheaded by former [[Wakefield Trinity]] owner Chris Brereton.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/17950216.chris-brereton-rumoured-head-consortium-taking-bulls/|title=Chris Brereton rumoured to head consortium taking over Bulls|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=6 October 2019 |accessdate=28 October 2019}}</ref> Prop forward [[Jon Magrin]] signed a one-year deal to play at [[Dewsbury Rams]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://dewsburyrams.co.uk/2019/10/04/magrin-joins-rams-on-one-year-deal/|title=Magrin joins Rams on One Year Deal|work=Dewsbury Rams |date=4 October 2019 |accessdate=28 October 2019}}</ref> Popular hard hitting centre [[Jake Webster]] signed a two-year deal with [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] side [[Keighley Cougars]] combining a playing contract alongside a role working with the community.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17955220.keighley-hope-webster-can-springboard-new-cougarmania/|title=Keighley hope Webster can be springboard for new Cougarmania|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=8 October 2019 |accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref> Potential new owner Brereton revealed that the clubs had debts of up to £500,000 and that he would work towards getting Bradford debt free in two years.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17970593.chris-brereton-aims-get-bradford-bulls-debt-free-two-years/|title=Chris Brereton aims to get Bradford Bulls debt free in two years|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=16 October 2019 |accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref> The exodus continued at the Bulls as fan favourite, Bradford born [[Ross Peltier]] signed for [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17977976.bradford-bulls-confirm-doncaster-departure-peltier/|title=Bradford Bulls confirm Doncaster departure for Peltier|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=18 October 2019 |accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref> Chris Brereton pulled out of the consortium attempting to buy the Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/17982227.breaking-prospective-bulls-buyer-brereton-walks-away/|title=Chris Brereton walks away but Bulls fans forums go ahead|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=21 October 2019 |accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref> However the following day it was revealed that former head of RFL Nigel Wood and Dewsbury chairman Mark Sawyer had appointed former [[Toronto Wolfpack]] CEO Eric Perez as the chairman to take over the consortium's bid to buy the Bulls, this brought the club out of special measures.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17986190.eric-perez-fronts-consortium-taking-bradford-bulls/|title=Consortium taking over Bulls appoint Eric Perez as chairman|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=22 October 2019 |accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref>

Following the news that the club had been lifted out of special measures it was announced that academy product [[Ross Oakes]] had signed a new two-year deal with the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/53439/oakes-signs-for-two-years|title=OAKES SIGNS FOR TWO YEARS|work=Bradford Bulls |date=24 October 2019 |accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref> However in order to raise some capital for the club playmaker [[Joe Keyes (rugby league)|Joe Keyes]], young second rower [[Matthew Storton]], second rower [[Elliot Minchella]], halfback [[Rowan Milnes]] and academy player Anesu Mudoti all signed three year deals at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] for an undisclosed fee with Milnes coming back to Bradford on loan for 2020.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17991848.five-bulls-aces-join-hull-kr-milnes-returns-loan/|title=Five Bulls aces join Hull KR but Milnes returns on loan|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=24 October 2019 |accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/53442/milnes-returns-on-loan-for-|title=MILNES RETURNS ON LOAN FOR The Bulls announced two pre-season friendlies for the upcoming season, the first will be against [[Castleford Tigers]] on Boxing Day and the second will be against [[Dewsbury Rams]] on 19 January.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/53495/bulls-make-jungle-trip-to-tackle-tigers|title=BULLS MAKE JUNGLE TRIP TO TACKLE TIGERS|work=Bradford Bulls |date=8 November 2019 |accessdate=17 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/53487/bulls-to-lock-horns-with-rams-in-pre-season-clash|title=BULLS TO LOCK HORNS WITH RAMS IN PRE-SEASON web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/movie-star-adam-fogerty-links-up-with-nigel-wood-again-at-bradford-bulls-1-10105964|title=Movie star Adam Fogerty links up with Nigel Wood first week, in addition to this Bradford would play their 'away' game against [[Dewsbury Rams]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley Stadium]] in a double header which will see [[Leeds Rhinos]] face off against [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18042266.bradford-bulls-face-broncos-home-opener-new-season/|title=Bradford Bulls face Broncos |accessdate=4 December 2019}}</ref>

===December 2019===

After a four year ban from the game it was announced that former [[Leigh Centurions]] prop forward [[Sam Barlow (rugby league)|Sam Barlow]] signed for the Bulls for the 2020 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/53573/barlow-is-a-bull-for-|title=BARLOW IS A BULL FOR 2020|work=Bradford Bulls |date=6 December 2019 |accessdate=13 December 2019}}</ref> Following this news it was revealed that [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] had let young second-row forward [[Adam Rooks]] join the Bulls on a season long loan.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/53596/bulls-recruit-rooks-to-bolster-pack-in-|title=BULLS RECRUIT ROOKS TO BOLSTER PACK IN 2020|work=Bradford Bulls |date=10 December 2019 |accessdate=13 December 2019}}</ref> A further signing was made to the Bulls roster as [[Racing Club Albi XIII]] prop [[Levy Nzoungou]] signed a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/53608/bradford-boost-as-levy-signs-for-|title=BRADFORD BOOST AS LEVY SIGNS FOR 2020|work=Bradford Bulls |date=12 December 2019 |accessdate=13 December 2019}}</ref> The Bulls rounded off the year with a 20–14 victory over [[Super League]] side [[Castleford Tigers]].

===January 2020===

The start of 2020 saw experienced centre [[Rhys Evans (rugby league)|Rhys Evans]] join [[Super League]] side [[Leeds Rhinos]] on a season long loan deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/2020/01/02/rhinos-sign-bulls-evans-on-loan/|title=RHINOS SIGN BULLS EVANS ON LOAN|work=Leeds Rhinos |date=2 January 2020 |accessdate=15 January 2020}}</ref> A young Bradford side traveled to [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.]] for the second pre-season friendly and lost a close match 18–26 with Murphy Smith, Harry Shackleton and Matthew Race all scoring for the Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18139078.march-report-hunslet-26-bradford-bulls-18/|title=MATCH REPORT: Hunslet 26 Bradford Bulls 18|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=5 January 2020 |accessdate=15 January 2020}}</ref> The Bulls announced the capture of former academy product [[Dan Waite-Pullan]] on a one-year deal from the Leeds Rhinos.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/519/waite-pullan-signs-one-year-deal-to-be-a-bull-in-2020|title=WAITE-PULLAN SIGNS ONE YEAR DEAL TO BE A BULL IN 2020|work=Bradford Bulls |date=10 January 2020 |accessdate=15 January 2020}}</ref> Bradford faced arch rivals Leeds in a pre-season friendly for [[Jamie Jones-Buchanan]]'s testimonial. However former Leeds player [[Rob Burrow]] was diagnosed with [[Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis|Motor Neurone Disease (MND)]] so the match was also used to raise funds to combat MND.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rob Burrow: Leeds Rhinos legend diagnosed with motor neurone disease|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/50854089 |website=BBC News |accessdate=15 January 2020}}</ref> The Bulls lost 34–10 in front of a 19,700 sell out crowd at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley Stadium]] with [[Ross Oakes]] and [[Thomas Doyle (rugby league)|Thomas Doyle]] scoring for the Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/report/leeds-rhinos-34-bradford-bulls-10/|title=LEEDS RHINOS 34 BRADFORD BULLS 10|work=Leeds Rhinos |date=12 January 2020 |accessdate=15 January 2020}}</ref> Former Bulls players and legends such as [[Wayne Godwin]], [[Matty Blythe]], [[Rob Parker (rugby league)|Rob Parker]], [[Nathan McAvoy]], [[Robbie Hunter-Paul]] and [[Stuart Fielden]] also featured in the final 10 minutes of the match in support of Rob Burrow.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18140982.bulls-announce-legends-will-take-field-leeds-friendly/|title=Bulls legends to don the shirt once again for Leeds friendly|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=6 January 2020 |accessdate=15 January 2020}}</ref> Super League side [[Hull F.C.]] revealed that they would partner up with the Bulls on dual registration for the 2020 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/535/bulls-join-forces-with-high-flying-airlie-birds|title=BULLS JOIN FORCES WITH HIGH FLYING AIRLIE BIRDS|work=Bradford Bulls |date=15 January 2020 |accessdate=15 January 2020}}</ref> The 2020 black away shirt was released and the 2020 squad numbers were also revealed for the Bulls with new signings [[Anthony England]], [[Adam Rooks]], [[Dan Waite-Pullan]] and [[Sam Barlow (rugby league)|Sam Barlow]] taking the 8, 11, 20 and 27 shirts respectively.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/531/bulls-reveal-2020-squad-numbers|title=BULLS REVEAL 2020 SQUAD NUMBERS|work=Bradford Bulls |date=15 January 2020 |accessdate=15 January 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18163165.bradford-bulls-reveal-away-kit-new-season/|title=Bradford Bulls reveal away kit for new season|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=15 January 2020 |accessdate=15 January 2020}}</ref> Another pre-season defeat followed this with a 25–12 loss to [[Dewsbury Rams]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18171625.sam-barlow-suffers-broken-arm-john-kear-unimpressed-bulls/|title=Sam Barlow suffers broken arm as John Kear unimpressed by Bulls|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=19 January 2020 |accessdate=22 January 2020}}</ref> The Bulls announced the signing of [[Hull F.C.]] winger [[Matty Dawson-Jones]] on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/546/matty-dawson-jones-becomes-a-bull|title=MATTY DAWSON-JONES BECOMES A BULL|work=Bradford Bulls |date=23 January 2020 |accessdate=1 February 2020}}</ref> In the final pre-season match the Bulls faced rivals [[York City Knights]] and came away with a hard-fought 6–4 win before the match was abandoned ten minutes before the final whistle.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18188487.match-report-bradford-bulls-6---york-city-knights-4/|title=Bradford Bulls overcome York in fierce final friendly|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=26 January 2020 |accessdate=1 February 2020}}</ref>

===February 2020===

The season commenced with a 14–18 loss to newly relegated side [[London Broncos]], new recruit [[Matty Dawson-Jones]] crossed for two tries on his debut whilst young hooker [[Thomas Doyle (rugby league)|Thomas Doyle]] also scored for the home side.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/bradford-vs-london/52911|title=BRADFORD BULLS 14 LONDON BRONCOS 18 |work=Sky Sports |date=2 February 2020 |accessdate=2 February 2020}}</ref> The Round 2 match against [[York City Knights]] was postponed due to bad weather due to the effects of [[Storm Ciara]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18222165.storm-ciara-sees-bradford-bulls-west-bowling-games-called-off/|title=Storm Ciara sees Bradford Bulls and West Bowling games called off |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=9 February 2020 |accessdate=17 February 2020}}</ref> The draw for the [[2020 Challenge Cup]] fourth round revealed that the Bulls would face amateur side [[Underbank Rangers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/51445100|title=Challenge Cup draw: Amateurs Underbank handed Bradford Bulls tie |work=BBC Sport |date=10 February 2020 |accessdate=17 February 2020}}</ref> A narrow 22–30 loss to promotion favourites [[Featherstone Rovers]] the following weekend saw the Bulls enter the season with back to back losses, Dawson-Jones was on the scoreboard along with Doyle, [[Brandon Pickersgill]] and [[Jordan Lilley]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/bradford-vs-featherstone/52927|title=BRADFORD BULLS 22 FEATHERSTONE ROVERS 30 |work=Sky Sports |date=16 February 2020 |accessdate=17 February 2020}}</ref> The Bulls progressed through to the 5th Round of the Challenge Cup with an uncompromising 22–0 win over amateurs Underbank Rangers, following this victory the Bulls were drawn away to [[Super League]] side [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/bradford-vs-underbank/53844|title=BRADFORD BULLS 22 UNDERBANK RANGERS 0 |work=Sky Sports |date=22 February 2020 |accessdate=27 February 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18258262.challenge-cup-bradford-bulls-drawn-away-wakefield-trinity/|title=Challenge Cup: Bradford Bulls drawn away to [[Leeds Rhinos]], however he was then loaned back to Bradford for the rest of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18266502.kear-delight-welcome-evans-back-bradford-bulls-plans/|title=Kear delight to welcome Evans back in Bradford Bulls plans |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=27 February 2020 |accessdate=27 February 2020}}</ref>

===March 2020===

March started positively for the Bulls as they overcame [[Oldham R.L.F.C.]] 26–12 to gain the first victory in the league, new loan signing [[Joe Cator]] led the way scoring the opening try of the game with [[Steve Crossley]], [[Sam Hallas]] and [[Brandon Pickersgill]] also scoring tries.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/oldham-vs-bradford/52933|title=OLDHAM 12 BRADFORD BULLS 26 |work=Sky Sports |date=1 March 2020 |accessdate=8 March 2020}}</ref> The Bulls bolstered their squad by signing former [[Featherstone Rovers]] hooker [[Danny Maskill]] who had been on trial in the Bulls reserve team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18279281.bulls-happy-hook-maskill/|title=Bulls happy to hook up with Maskill |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=4 March 2020 |accessdate=8 March 2020}}</ref> Bradford followed up their first Championship win with a solid 28–0 victory over [[Sheffield Eagles]], young academy product opened up his senior try scoring account with a brace of tries whilst [[Jordan Lilley]], [[Matty Dawson-Jones]] and Pickersgill also scored.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/bradford-vs-sheffield/52937|title=BRADFORD BULLS 28 SHEFFIELD EAGLES 0 |work=Sky Sports |date=8 March 2020 |accessdate=22 June 2020}}</ref> The Bulls [[2020 Challenge Cup]] run came to an end in a hard fought 14–17 loss to [[Super League]] side [[Wakefield Trinity]], the Bulls were ahead with tries from Jordan Lilley and [[Rowan Milnes]] before Wakefield secured their place in the next round with a late try.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/wakefield-vs-bradford/53881|title=WAKEFIELD TRINITY 17 BRADFORD BULLS 14 |work=Sky Sports |date=13 March 2020 |accessdate=22 June 2020}}</ref> During this month all of rugby league including Super League and the [[RFL Championship]] was suspended indefinitely due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic in the United Kingdom]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/51911290|title=Coronavirus: Rugby Football League suspends season until 3 April |work=BBC Sport |date=16 March 2020 |accessdate=22 June 2020}}</ref>

===April 2020===

There was no rugby played throughout April due to the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic. At the start of the month the Bradford Bulls announced that the majority of their staff would be placed on furlough meaning that the government would cover 80% of employees salaries in accordance to the COVID-19 job retention scheme set out by the government.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18356156.bradford-bulls-put-majority-staff-furlough/|title=Bradford Bulls put majority of staff on furlough |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=3 April 2020 |accessdate=22 June 2020}}</ref>

===May 2020===

There was no rugby played throughout May due to the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic. The [[RFL]] announced that the [[Challenge Cup]] and [[RFL 1895 Cup|1895 Cup]] finals would be postponed to a later date in 2020 subject to government guidelines due to the coronavirus pandemic.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18459414.challenge-cup-1895-cup-finals-postponed/|title=Challenge Cup and 1895 Cup finals both postponed |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=19 May 2020 |accessdate=22 June 2020}}</ref>

===June 2020===

There was no rugby played throughout June due to the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic. The first piece of good news for the Bulls during the ongoing pandemic was the announcement that coach [[John Kear]] had signed a two-year extension to stay at the club until the end of the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/bradford-bulls-secure-john-kear-new-deal-2872995|title=Bradford Bulls secure John Kear on new deal |work=Yorkshire Post |date=3 June 2020 |accessdate=22 June 2020}}</ref> In a major coup for the club it was announced that veteran [[Super League]] halfback [[Danny Brough]] would join the Bulls in 2021 on a two-year deal from [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18534785.bulls-sign-experienced-half-back-brough-two-year-deal/|title=Bulls to sign experienced half back Brough on two-year deal |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=23 June 2020 |accessdate=24 June 2020}}</ref>

===July 2020===

There was no rugby played throughout July due to the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic. Vice captain [[Sam Hallas]] signed a one-year deal to stay at the club for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18573893.vice-captain-hallas-agrees-one-year-deal-bradford-bulls/|title=Vice-captain Hallas agrees one-year deal with Bradford Bulls |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=10 July 2020 |accessdate=14 July 2020}}</ref> During this month the [[Rugby Football League]] announced that the [[Championship (rugby league)|RFL Championship]] and [[League 1 (rugby league)|RFL League 1]] would not be able to continue the 2020 season, however an autumn tournament would be announced with a £250,000 prize fund to honor the 125th anniversary of the [[RFL]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18596120.bradford-bulls-want-take-part-rfl-125-year-tournament/|title=Bradford Bulls want to take part in RFL 125-year tournament |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=21 July 2020 |accessdate=27 July 2020}}</ref> Homegrown fullback [[Brandon Pickersgill]] put pen to paper on a new one-year deal to stay with the club for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18604283.full-back-pickersgill-signs-new-deal-bradford-bulls/|title=Full back Pickersgill signs new deal with Bradford Bulls |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=24 July 2020 |accessdate=27 July 2020}}</ref> Winger [[Joe Brown (rugby league)|Joe Brown]] also signed an extension to stay with the Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18619346.versatile-brown-signs-new-one-year-deal-bradford-bulls/|title=Versatile Brown signs new one-year deal with Bradford Bulls |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=31 July 2020 |accessdate=3 August 2020}}</ref>

===August 2020===

Due to the reserves being suspended until 2022 young halfback [[Cobi Green]] signed for [[Swinton Lions]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/swinton-sign-cobi-green-as-richard-lepori-extends-his-stay/|title=Swinton Sign Cobi Green |work=Love Rugby League |date=3 August 2020 |accessdate=18 August 2020}}</ref> Soon after Green's departure the Bulls announced the re-signing of second-row forwards [[Brad Gallagher]] and [[Ebon Scurr]], Gallagher signed a two-year extension whilst Scurr signed for one year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18634934.bradford-bulls-second-row-signs-deal-end-2022/|title=Bradford Bulls sign second row forward until the end of 2022 |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=7 August 2020 |accessdate=18 August 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18645397.prop-scurr-signs-new-one-year-deal-stay-bradford-bulls/|title=Prop Scurr signs new one-year deal to stay at Bradford Bulls |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=14 August 2020 |accessdate=18 August 2020}}</ref> Prop [[Levy Nzoungou]] also signed an extension with the Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18659715.nzoungou-signs-new-deal-bradford-bulls-next-season/|title=Nzoungou signs new deal with Bradford Bulls for next season |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=18 August 2020 |accessdate=18 August 2020}}</ref> Young hooker [[Thomas Doyle (rugby league)|Thomas Doyle]] was the next player to sign an extension with the Bulls, signing on for another season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18663414.another-bulls-deal-done-hooker-doyle-signs-2021/|title=Another Bulls deal done as hooker Doyle signs for 2021 |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=20 August 2020 |accessdate=20 August 2020}}</ref>

==Milestones==

*'''Round 1:''' [[Greg Johnson (rugby league)|Greg Johnson]], [[Matty Dawson-Jones]], [[Anthony England]], [[Adam Rooks]] and [[Levy Nzoungou]] made their debuts for the Bulls.
*'''Round 1:''' [[Brandon Pickersgill]] and [[Jordan Lilley]] made their 50th appearance for the Bulls.
*'''Round 1:''' [[Matty Dawson-Jones]] scored his 1st try for the Bulls.
*'''CCR4:''' [[Dan Waite-Pullan]] made his debut for the Bulls.
*'''CCR4:''' [[Levy Nzoungou]] and [[Adam Rooks]] scored their 1st try for the Bulls.
*'''Round 4:''' [[Joe Cator]] and [[Jack Brown (rugby league)|Jack Brown]] made their debuts for the Bulls.
*'''Round 4:''' [[Joe Cator]] scored his 1st try for the Bulls.
*'''Round 5:''' [[Brad Gallagher]] scored his 1st try for the Bulls.
